channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Quality of Life & PROs
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: Quality of Life & PROs

item.title: Unraveling microbial signatures in the comorbidity of autoimmune diseases and depression.
item.description: BMC microbiology - Depression is a common comorbidity in autoimmune diseases (ADs), including inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), a well-characterized AD with prominent gut involvement and strong host-microbiome intera...

item.title: Acid-enhanced drinkable hydrogel co-delivering riboflavin-loaded nanozymes for orchestrated inflammation-immune modulation and redox homeostasis in colitis therapy.
item.description: Journal of nanobiotechnology - Conventional inflammatory bowel disease therapies are limited by inefficient colon-targeted delivery, systemic side effects, and a failure to rectify the underlying oxidative stress and immune dysr...

item.title: Oral nanoarray hydrogel-mediated macrophage repolarization and ferroptosis suppression for inflammatory bowel disease treatment.
item.description: Journal of controlled release : official journal of the Controlled Release Society - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is closely associated with macrophage dysregulation, particularly insufficient anti-inflammatory M2 macrophages. However, current therapies mainly emphasize M2 pola...

item.title: Capsaicin attenuates DSS-induced IBD by suppressing macrophage M1 polarization via inhibition of the TRAF1/NF-κB pathway.
item.description: European journal of pharmacology - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a chronic inflammatory disorder of the intestine caused by multiple factors, with a global incidence that is increasing year by year, severely affecting patients...

item.title: Effectiveness of THC-containing cannabis for inflammatory bowel disease: a systematic review.
item.description: Journal of cannabis research - Systematic review - Patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) increasingly use cannabis. Studies investigating the effectiveness and safety of cannabis as a therapeutic option for IBD have reported conflicting fi...

item.title: Tuft cells protect against intestinal inflammation through histone deacetylase 3.
item.description: Mucosal immunology - While intestinal tuft cells are well known to promote type 2 immunity, their role in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) remains unclear. Here, we show that tuft cells are reduced in the distal intest...

item.title: Randomized Trial of Intensive Nurse-Led Follow‑Up Versus Standard Care in Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Digestive diseases and sciences - Nurses play a key role in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) management. This randomized controlled trial evaluated intensive nurse-led program in patients with IBD starting advanced therapy. Patie...

item.title: Privacy preserving digital platform for patient reported outcomes in inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: NPJ digital medicine - We developed a privacy-preserving digital platform for patient-reported outcomes in inflammatory bowel disease using secure multi-party computation (SMPC), which encrypts and fragments data to ensu...

item.title: Nicotinamide N-methyltransferase in inflammatory bowel disease: Multidimensional regulation, mechanistic insights, and therapeutic potential.
item.description: Experimental cell research - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a chronic intestinal inflammatory disorder involving immune, metabolic, microbial, and epigenetic dysregulation. Nicotinamide N-methyltransferase (NNMT) connects...

item.title: Pathological accumulation of aged neutrophils contributes to intestinal inflammation in inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Clinical science (London, England : 1979) - Neutrophils play a dual role in inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD), contributing to both host defense and tissue damage. Among them, CXCR4⁺CD62Llo aged neutrophils have been implicated in chronic in...

item.title: Small peptide encoded by MALAT1 aggravates colitis via upregulating GIP expression in enterocyte.
item.description: Biochemical pharmacology - Inflammatory bowel disease is a chronic and relapsing intestinal disorder that is challenging to cure. Enterocytes secrete various small peptides and are closely associated with IBD pathogenesis, b...

item.title: Theories and determinants of self-care and self-efficacy in inflammatory bowel diseases: A systematic review.
item.description: Patient education and counseling - Review - Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD) is a chronic condition requiring complex, lifelong self-care. While self-care and self-efficacy are critical for improving patient outcomes, the theoretical underpi...

item.title: Metabolic host-microbe crosstalk in stabilization of epithelial HIF.
item.description: Microbiome - The gastrointestinal tract is home to trillions of microorganisms that interact with their host in profound ways. One mechanism by which these microbes interact with their eukaryotic host is throug...

item.title: Patient-reported Outcomes in Clinical Trials of Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis.
item.description: Clinical gastroenterology and hepatology : the official clinical practice journal of the American Gastroenterological Association - Review - Patient-reported outcomes (PROs) and outcome measures (PROMs) have increasing prominence in clinical trials for inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). This systematic review provides an overview of PROM...

item.title: Fatigue in inflammatory bowel disease: Mechanisms, assessment, and management strategies.
item.description: Saudi journal of gastroenterology : official journal of the Saudi Gastroenterology Association - Fatigue is among the most prevalent and disabling symptoms in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), affecting a substantial proportion of patients during active disease and persisting in many despite c...

item.title: Nanogel-coated nanozymes for ulcerative colitis targeted treatment by restoring redox homeostasis and anti-inflammatory activity.
item.description: Colloids and surfaces. B, Biointerfaces - Excessive accumulation of reactive oxygen species (ROS) and overloaded immune system are key pathological features of ulcerative colitis (UC), but conventional treatments (such as aminosalicylates ...

item.title: Knockout of PARP2 in T cells leads to spontaneous colitis with distinct segmental characteristics in PARP1-deficient mice.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Suppression of poly(ADP-ribose) polymerase (PARP) activity, either PARP1 or PARP2, was proven to be beneficial in experimental colitis. The absence of PARP2 exclusively in T cells (T-PARP2-KO) was ...
